channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Instagram courts TikTok creators with cash bonuses up to $50,000
item.description: TikTok is not only battling for survival this week but also facing fierce competition from rivals eager to poach its top influencers. Among them is Meta’s Instagram, reportedly offering cash bonuses to lure big TikTok creators to its platform, Reels.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Elon Musk Clashes with OpenAI CEO Over Stargate AI Project
item.description: The public clash over the Stargate artificial intelligence infrastructure project is part of a years-long dispute between Elon Musk and OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, which began with a boardroom rivalry over who should run OpenAI.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Why some Instagram users are suddenly following Trump, Vance
item.description: Meta on Wednesday pushed back against claims its platforms were automatically forcing users to follow the Instagram accounts of President Trump and Vice President Vance following Inauguration Day.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Facebook’s group moderators are its last, best hope
item.description: By now, you may be sick of reading hot takes on Mark Zuckerberg’s decision to end Meta’s formal fact-checking activities, loosen its properties’ restrictions on hate speech, and retool itself for the Trump 2.0 era. So I promise I’m not going to burden you with yet another one.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: We celebrate the Samsung Galaxy S25 launch event with a special episode of our podcast
item.description: Let's talk the new Samsung Galaxy S25, and everything else that was announced at Samsung's Unpacked 2025 event.
item.dc:creator: hamish.hector@futurenet.com (Hamish Hector), TechRadar

item.title: Wisconsin man accused of setting fire to congressman's office over TikTok ban charged with arson
item.description: Prosecutors have charged a Wisconsin man who allegedly told police he tried to burn down a congressman's office because he was upset over the federal TikTok ban with felony arson.
item.dc:creator: Todd Richmond, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: MrBeast not quite in the bidding race for TikTok just yet, rep says
item.description: Jimmy Donaldson — better known online as MrBeast — isn’t in the TikTok bidding race just yet, according to a representative for the YouTube star.
item.dc:creator: James Pollard, Haleluya Hadero And Wyatte Grantham-Philips, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: For the first time ever, I wish Google would act more like Amazon
item.description: Fair warning: This isn’t your average article about what’s happening with all the newfangled AI hullabaloo in this weird and wild world of ours.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 3 promises every CIO should keep in 2025
item.description: Nearly all tech surprises last year were related to gen AI, which was so hyped in 2023 that every organization had to try it in one or more projects in 2024.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: 3 Python web frameworks for beautiful front ends
item.description: Python has long had a presence as a language for server-side frameworks, with support for most every project size or use case. But it’s historically been confined to the back end—there’s no real culture of writing Python for creating front-end, client-side code. At least not yet.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How to deal with a Big Pile of Mud
item.description: Last week I talked about where those big, unwieldy code bases come from. They don’t appear out of nowhere, and no one ever plans to create one.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Trump to announce $500 billion private sector AI infrastructure investment
item.description: President Donald Trump is due to announce private sector investment of up to $500 billion of artificial intelligence infrastructure on Tuesday, two sources told Reuters.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Trump administration shuts down White House Spanish-language page and social media
item.description: Within hours of President Donald Trump’s inauguration, his administration took down the Spanish version of the official White House website.
item.dc:creator: Fernanda Figueroa, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Why Trump dashed Biden’s AI executive order, and why it matters
item.description: During President Donald Trump’s first few hours in power, he revoked Joe Biden’s 2023 executive order on AI. In the absence of congressional action, Biden’s EO served as the first effort by the federal government to address the considerable societal and national security risks presented by AI.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Trump pardons founder of Silk Road website
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump announced Tuesday that he had pardoned Ross Ulbricht, the founder of Silk Road, an underground website for selling drugs.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump open to Elon Musk buying TikTok
item.description: TikTok restored service to U.S. users Sunday afternoon and credited Trump for pushing to keep the app operational.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Crenshaw pushes Apple to use 'Gulf of America' on maps
item.description: Rep. Dan Crenshaw (R-Texas) urged Apple to officially change the name of the Gulf of Mexico on its mapping platform following President Trump’s Monday executive order declaring the land mass the Gulf of America. “Hey @tim_cook, just noticed Apple Maps still calls it the Gulf of Mexico.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: AI Revolutionizes Cloud Security: Embracing a New Era of Defense and Offense
item.description: Cloud security is undergoing a significant transformation with the integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI). While AI enhances defense capabilities, attackers utilize AI to launch sophisticated attacks, creating an 'arms race' between defenders and attackers. This article delves into the implications of AI on cloud security and provides recommendations for organizations to stay ahead of the curve.
item.dc:creator: Info World
